objective: Create a production-grade point-in-time data acquisition contract and dry-run harvester plan for historical index constituents/weights and broad A-share stock daily raw/adjusted data before any new factor or model harness is opened.
forbidden: No portfolio harness or strategy backtest.
No model promotion or signal promotion.
Do not write real credentials to repository files or reports.
Do not scrape browser login cookies into API credentials.
Do not mark dry-run plans as acquired data.
Do not approve historical constituents/weights until actual dated vintages pass PIT validation.
acceptance_criteria: The contract contains dataset, provider, endpoint, required fields, output path, availability rule, and PIT validation gates.
Priority 2 covers broad stock daily raw bars, daily basic data, adjustment factors, listing status, pause/ST/limit flags, and delisted names.
Dry-run readiness clearly reports missing SDKs or credentials without revealing secrets.
The task emits a next executable handoff without running model code.
failure_conditions: Any dataset is described as acquired when only dry-run was executed.
Credentials or tokens appear in output files.
Current snapshots are approved as historical data.
quality_gates: available_date or effective_date is mandatory for historical constituents, weights, financials, and classifications.
raw_close, adjusted_close, and adj_factor must be separated for stock daily data.
stock universe must include listed, delisted, and paused names when provider supports them.
dry_run_mode must be true for this task run.
next_handoff: If credentials and SDKs are available, data_steward may run the harvester in execute mode for a small pilot batch first; otherwise the next task is credential/SDK setup, not factor research.
